name: Data Models & Schemas
description: Frozen Pydantic v2 models and StrEnums forming the shared data contract for the entire Exemplar system. Provides: _ExemplarBase (shared frozen base with canonical_bytes()/canonical_hash() for deterministic hashing), 9 enums (Severity, Confidence, ReviewStage, ReviewDecision, ClassificationLabel, ChroniclerEventType, LearnerPhase, StigmergyVerb, CliExitCode), and 16 domain models in dependency order (ClassificationLabel → DiffHunk → ReviewRequest → Finding → Assessment → TrustScore → TesseraSeal → ReviewReport → ReviewerCredential → PolicyToken → ChroniclerEvent → StigmergySignal → LearningRecord → KindexEntry → PipelineResult). All models use frozen=True and extra='forbid'. Shallow-freeze semantics with list[]/dict[] fields. IsoTimestamp annotated type for ISO 8601 validation. JSON round-trip guarantee: M.model_validate_json(instance.model_dump_json()) == instance. PACT_KEY ClassVar on base model for Sentinel attribution. No ABC per SOP — concrete Pydantic base only. Module-level ordering functions for enums with comparison semantics. __all__ export control for clean imports.

types.name: IsoTimestamp
types.description: Annotated str type validated against ISO 8601 format. Stored as plain str but guaranteed to parse as a valid ISO 8601 datetime string. Uses AfterValidator to reject non-conforming values at model construction time.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Severity level of a review finding, ordered from most to least critical.

types.name: Confidence
types.description: Confidence level of a reviewer finding or merged assessment.

types.name: ReviewStage
types.description: Named stage in the review circuit pipeline.

types.name: ReviewDecision
types.description: Final decision on a pull request review: block merging, warn, or pass.

types.name: ClassificationLabel
types.description: Ledger data classification label for diff content.

types.name: ChroniclerEventType
types.description: Well-known Chronicler event types emitted throughout the review lifecycle.

types.name: LearnerPhase
types.description: Apprentice learning phase progression stage.

types.name: StigmergyVerb
types.description: Stigmergy signal verb for inter-agent coordination. StrEnum.

types.name: CliExitCode
types.description: CLI process exit codes mapping to review outcomes.

types.name: _ExemplarBase
types.description: Shared frozen Pydantic v2 base model. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Provides canonical_bytes() and canonical_hash() methods. Contains PACT_KEY: ClassVar[str] = 'exemplar.schemas' for Sentinel attribution. All domain models inherit from this.

types.name: DiffHunk

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this hunk (deterministic from content).

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Relative file path the hunk belongs to.

fields.name: start_line_old
fields.description: Starting line number in the old file.

fields.name: count_old
fields.description: Number of lines in the old file span.

fields.name: start_line_new
fields.description: Starting line number in the new file.

fields.name: count_new
fields.description: Number of lines in the new file span.

fields.name: context_before
fields.description: Context lines before the change.

fields.name: added_lines
fields.description: Lines added in this hunk.

fields.name: removed_lines
fields.description: Lines removed in this hunk.

fields.name: context_after
fields.description: Context lines after the change.

fields.name: raw_header
fields.description: Original @@ header line from the diff.

fields.name: classifications
fields.description: Ledger classification labels applied to this hunk.

fields.name: language
fields.description: Detected programming language of the file, if known.

types.description: A single parsed hunk from a unified diff, with metadata and classification labels.

types.name: ReviewRequest

fields.name: id

fields.name: source

fields.name: metadata

types.name: TesseraSeal

fields.name: content_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON content that was sealed.

fields.name: previous_hash
fields.description: Hash of the previous seal in the chain (null for first seal).

fields.name: chain_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of (content_hash + previous_hash), forming the chain link.

fields.name: sealed_at
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the seal was created.

fields.name: sealer_id
fields.description: Identifier of the entity that created this seal.

types.description: A hash-chain tamper-proof seal over serialized content, verifiable independently.

functions.name: severity_rank
functions.description: Returns an integer rank for a Severity enum value. Higher rank = more severe. critical=4, high=3, medium=2, low=1, info=0. Pure function, no side effects. Used for sorting and comparison of severity levels across the system.

error_cases.name: invalid_severity
error_cases.condition: severity is not a valid Severity enum member

preconditions: severity must be a valid Severity enum member

functions.name: confidence_rank
functions.description: Returns an integer rank for a Confidence enum value. Higher rank = more confident. high=2, medium=1, low=0. Pure function, no side effects.

error_cases.name: invalid_confidence
error_cases.condition: confidence is not a valid Confidence enum member

preconditions: confidence must be a valid Confidence enum member

functions.name: learner_phase_rank
functions.description: Returns an integer rank for a LearnerPhase enum value. Higher rank = more advanced phase. observe=0, suggest=1, act=2, reflect=3. Pure function, no side effects.

inputs.name: phase
inputs.description: The learner phase value to rank.

error_cases.name: invalid_phase
error_cases.condition: phase is not a valid LearnerPhase enum member

preconditions: phase must be a valid LearnerPhase enum member

functions.name: canonical_bytes
functions.description: Instance method on _ExemplarBase. Returns deterministic JSON bytes of the model instance suitable for SHA-256 hashing. Uses model_dump(mode='json') serialized with sort_keys=True and compact separators (',', ':'). The output is stable across Python dict insertion order and is used by TesseraSeal for hash-chain proofs. PACT_KEY attribution: docstring references _ExemplarBase.PACT_KEY for Sentinel tracing.
preconditions: self is a valid _ExemplarBase subclass instance that passes Pydantic validation
postconditions: output is valid UTF-8 encoded JSON
output is deterministic: identical field values always produce identical bytes
json.loads(output) produces a dict equivalent to self.model_dump(mode='json')
calling canonical_bytes() twice on the same instance returns identical bytes

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total number of fields recursively

functions.name: canonical_hash
functions.description: Instance method on _ExemplarBase. Returns the hex SHA-256 digest of canonical_bytes(). Used by TesseraSeal.content_hash and TesseraSeal.seal_hash for hash-chain integrity verification. PACT_KEY attribution: docstring references _ExemplarBase.PACT_KEY for Sentinel tracing.
preconditions: self is a valid _ExemplarBase subclass instance that passes Pydantic validation
postconditions: output is a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string
output is deterministic for identical field values

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total number of fields recursively

functions.name: validate_iso_timestamp
functions.description: Validator function for the IsoTimestamp annotated type. Accepts a string, validates it conforms to ISO 8601 datetime format (using datetime.fromisoformat()), and returns the original string unchanged. Used as an AfterValidator in the Annotated type definition.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The string value to validate as ISO 8601.

error_cases.name: invalid_iso_format
error_cases.condition: value cannot be parsed by datetime.fromisoformat()

error_data.detail: Timestamp must be valid ISO 8601 format

postconditions: returned value is identical to input value (no transformation)

invariants: All models inherit from _ExemplarBase which sets frozen=True and extra='forbid' via ConfigDict
No model uses ABC or abstract methods — only concrete Pydantic BaseModel per SOP
All models satisfy JSON round-trip: M.model_validate_json(instance.model_dump_json()) == instance
canonical_bytes() output is deterministic: same field values always produce identical bytes regardless of insertion order
canonical_hash() returns SHA-256 hex digest of canonical_bytes()
Models are defined in dependency order so no ForwardRef annotations are needed
All list[] and dict[] fields use shallow-freeze semantics — the container is not deeply frozen
IsoTimestamp fields accept only valid ISO 8601 strings and store them as str
PACT_KEY ClassVar is present on _ExemplarBase and inherited by all models
StrEnum is used for all text-valued enums; IntEnum only for CliExitCode
Every public type is listed in __all__

data_access.rationale: Schemas are pure data definitions with no persistent storage. Models may hold fields classified as INTERNAL or PII (e.g., reviewer identity in ReviewerCredential, file paths in DiffHunk), but the schemas module itself only reads PUBLIC configuration data (enum definitions, model structure). It does not read or write to any external data store, database, or file system. All data flows through these types in-memory only.

side_effects.rationale: canonical_bytes() and canonical_hash() produce deterministic SHA-256 digests of model instances for hash-chain proofs (TesseraSeal) and deduplication. These are pure CPU computations with no I/O.

authority.rationale: This component is the single source of truth for all domain types, enums, and serialization contracts used across every other Exemplar component. No other component may define or redefine these data structures. All inter-component data exchange uses types defined here.
